untranslatable_reason: This signal requires external market data not in the allowed indicator set: (1) 'incremental return from futures arbitrage' — a computed spread/differential between stock-futures pricing that is not available as a standard price indicator, (2) 'T-bill return' — a fixed-income instrument yield external to the stock/futures universe. The signal is fundamentally about comparing two different asset classes and their returns, which cannot be expressed using only price action, technical indicators, and volume metrics on a single instrument. This is a macro/portfolio allocation decision rather than a technical entry/exit rule.
translation_notes: MCMILLAN_DRY_249 describes an arbitrage opportunity selection rule that compares the relative attractiveness of stocks-versus-futures arbitrage against alternative fixed-income instruments. Both key parameters (arbitrage return differential and T-bill return threshold) are marked AUTHOR_SILENT with no numerical guidance provided in the source text. The decision logic is quantitative but requires external data feeds (futures basis, T-bill yields) outside the scope of single-instrument technical indicators. To implement this rule, a backtest engine would need custom data sources for (a) basis spread between spot and futures, and (b) current T-bill yield, plus a user-configured return threshold.
